texto: Under a bad leadership Serbs are capable of committing the most terrible atrocities; under good leaders we can do great deeds. It's like a field - if it's not cared for, the weeds will take over. But if you tend it, water and feed the seeds, you will read a bountiful harvest. Serbs are lazy, we lack discipline and have no capacity for self-criticism." With Their Backs to the World
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Åsne Seierstad: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a lideranca. O trecho “Under a bad leadership Serbs are capable of committing the most terrible atrocities; under good l…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que lideranca, discipline, leadership pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e leadership.

texto: In Afghanistan a woman’s longing for love is taboo. It is forbidden by the tribes’ notion of honor and by the mullahs. Young people have no right to meet, to love, or to choose. Love has little to do with romance; on the contrary, love can be interpreted as committing a serious crime, punishable by death.
explicacao: Citação de Åsne Seierstad sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “In Afghanistan a woman’s longing for love is taboo. It is forbidden by the tribes’ notion of hono…” articula amor, love-hurts, reflexao em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao.

Love has little to do with romance; on the contrary, love can be interpreted as committing a serious crime, punishable by death.

texto: Be yourself!’ said Gro. ‘No one will hear any of you otherwise, still less trust you. That’s the most important thing of all. If you’re not yourself you just can’t sustain it in the long term.
